Lady
Cerelle Lannister
Cerelle Lannister, by dreamsofdany ©
Titles
  • Lady of Casterly Rock[1]
  • Shield of Lannisport[1]
  • Warden of the West[1]
Allegiance House Lannister
Culture Westermen
Born 209 AC[2]
Died 213 AC[2]
Father Lord Tybolt Lannister
Mother Lady Teora Kyndall
Book The World of Ice & Fire (mentioned)

Cerelle Lannister was the Lady of Casterly Rock and the head of House Lannister during the beginning of King Aerys I Targaryen's reign.[1]

Contents

  • 1 History
  • 2 Family
  • 3 Behind the Scenes
  • 4 References

History[ ]

Cerelle was the only child of Lord Tybolt Lannister and his wife, Lady Teora Kyndall. When Tybolt died in 212 AC, Cerelle succeeded him as the Lady of Casterly Rock, at the age of three. Her uncle, Gerold Lannister, served as her regent.[1]

Cerelle's rule would last for less than a year, as she herself suddenly passed away in 213 AC. After her death, she was succeeded by her uncle Gerold as Lord of Casterly Rock. Since both Lady Cerelle and her father Tybolt died suddenly and under suspicious circumstances, it was whispered that their deaths had been caused by Gerold, so that he would inherit the rule of House Lannister and the westerlands.[1] According to a semi-canon source, the most common tale was that Gerold had murdered his niece himself, smothering her with a pillow as she slept.[3]

Gerold ruled the westerlands for thirty-one years, earning the sobriquet Gerold the Golden. Despite all his accomplishments, some of his lords and many of his smallfolk had no love for him, as they blamed him for his niece's death. According to a semi-canon source, Lady Ellyn Tarbeck later named a daughter after Lady Cerelle to slight Gerold.[3]

Notes:
  1. ↑ In 298 AC, it has been roughly ninety years since Gowen and Tya were wed, placing their marriage around 208 AC. Therefore Tya was a contemporary to Lords Damon (d.210 AC) and Tybolt (d.212 AC).

Behind the Scenes[ ]

In the unabridged The World of Ice & Fire Westerlands section on George R.R. Martin's website, her name is spelled "Cyrelle", as is Ellyn Tarbeck's daughter.[3]
